reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of in-situ-generated nitrile oxide with phenylacetylene to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C-O and C-C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N-O bond rearrangement in ethyl nitroacetate during nitrile oxide generation

feed_streams.reasoning: All reagents are miscible in the DES and no incompatibility exists between phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate at room temperature; nitrile oxide is only generated at elevated temperature. Pre-mixing in a single feed simplifies pumping of the viscous DES. Using a single stream avoids the challenge of metering two viscous DES streams.

reagents.name: phenylacetylene
reagents.notes: Terminal alkyne substrate for regioselective cycloaddition

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide in situ at 120°C via dehydration; 2 equiv used to ensure full conversion of dipolarophile

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.equiv_or_loading: 1:5 molar ratio with EG; 1 mL total DES per 0.5 mmol substrate
reagents.notes: Forms deep eutectic solvent with ethylene glycol; acts as reaction medium promoting nitrile oxide formation without added base or catalyst

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.notes: Hydrogen bond donor in DES; contributes to thermal stability and solvation

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal dehydration/tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide (EtO2C-CNO) in situ. The DES medium facilitates this transformation without external base.

Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide (1,3-dipole) with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate regioselectively. The oxygen of the nitrile oxide bonds to the terminal carbon of the alkyne, and the carbon of the nitrile oxide bonds to the internal carbon.

stream_logic.reasoning: All components are pre-mixed in a single DES solution. No incompatibility at room temperature — nitrile oxide is only generated upon heating to 120°C in the reactor. Single-stream approach avoids the difficulty of metering and mixing two viscous DES feeds. Concentration: phenylacetylene at 0.5 M, ethyl nitroacetate at 1.0 M in 1 mL DES per 0.5 mmol substrate.

mixing_order_reasoning: Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate are both stable at room temperature and can be dissolved together in the DES before entering the heated reactor. The nitrile oxide intermediate is only generated at 120°C, so premixing is safe and ensures the dipolarophile is immediately available to trap the reactive nitrile oxide, minimizing dimerization to furoxan.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species or radicals involved; thermal pericyclic mechanism is unaffected by O2.
quench_reasoning: The batch protocol quenches with water for workup/extraction purposes only. In flow, the product stream can be collected directly and worked up offline. No reactive intermediates persist after the 15 min residence time at 120°C (99% conversion reported).
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism and flow translation. Key uncertainties: (1) DES viscosity at room temperature may require heated feed lines or dilution for reliable pumping — TBAB/EG DES is moderately viscous; a syringe pump or HPLC pump with heated inlet may be needed. (2) The 120°C temperature exceeds the boiling point of phenylacetylene (142°C at 1 atm) but is below it, so a back-pressure regulator (BPR, ~5-10 bar) is recommended to prevent bubble formation. (3) The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate suggests incomplete nitrile oxide generation efficiency; excess is needed to drive full conversion. (4) Water is generated as a by-product of nitrile oxide formation but is miscible with the DES and should not cause issues.
